name: Slow PWM Output
description: Similar to PWM, the Slow PWM Output platform allows you to control GPIO pins by pulsing them on/off over a longer time period. It could be used to control a heating element through a relay where a fast PWM update cycle would not be appropriate.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The id to use for this output component.

config_entries.label: Period
config_entries.description: The duration of each cycle. (i.e. a 10s period at 50% duty would result in the pin being turned on for 5s, then off for 5s)

config_entries.label: Restart Cycle On State Change
config_entries.description: Restart a timer of a cycle when new state is set. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: State Change Action
config_entries.description: An automation to perform when the load is switched. If a lambda is used the boolean `state` parameter holds the new status.

config_entries.label: Turn Off Action
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `turn_on_action` (all-or-none).

An automation to perform when the load is turned off. `turn_on_action` and `turn_off_action` must be configured together.

config_entries.label: Turn On Action
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `turn_off_action` (all-or-none).

An automation to perform when the load is turned on. Can be used to control for example a switch or output component.
